texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 1. Executive Summary
texts.text: 1. Executive Summary

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Overall actual progress reached 34.2% against planned 35.5% . Status is now Amber/Red due to widening schedule drag at Cheras North Station , compounded by heavy rainfall, utility conflict escalation, and slower-than-planned precast segment output.
texts.text: Overall actual progress reached 34.2% against planned 35.5% . Status is now Amber/Red due to widening schedule drag at Cheras North Station , compounded by heavy rainfall, utility conflict escalation, and slower-than-planned precast segment output.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The package remains recoverable, but current trend indicates material schedule pressure unless three actions are completed in March:
texts.text: The package remains recoverable, but current trend indicates material schedule pressure unless three actions are completed in March:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 1. utility authority approval is escalated and secured
texts.text: utility authority approval is escalated and secured

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2. alternate precast capacity is activated
texts.text: alternate precast capacity is activated

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 3. phased interface approvals are accepted by the client team
texts.text: phased interface approvals are accepted by the client team

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 3A. Milestone Watchlist
texts.text: 3A. Milestone Watchlist

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 4. Key Developments
texts.text: 4. Key Developments

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 4.1 Weather Impact
texts.text: 4.1 Weather Impact

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Persistent rainfall during February reduced productive time for pier erection and constrained concrete operations at open work fronts. The team resequenced several activities, but the lost productivity was only partially recovered.
texts.text: Persistent rainfall during February reduced productive time for pier erection and constrained concrete operations at open work fronts. The team resequenced several activities, but the lost productivity was only partially recovered.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 4.2 Cheras North Utility Conflict
texts.text: 4.2 Cheras North Utility Conflict

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Additional underground services were identified in the Cheras North corridor, requiring redesign of the immediate work sequence and closer coordination with utility owners. The site team expects diversion completion in March, subject to authority clearance.
texts.text: Additional underground services were identified in the Cheras North corridor, requiring redesign of the immediate work sequence and closer coordination with utility owners. The site team expects diversion completion in March, subject to authority clearance.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 4.3 Supply Chain
texts.text: 4.3 Supply Chain

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The precast segment supplier reported a temporary reduction in weekly output caused by mould maintenance and labour shortage. This has not yet become a critical path failure, but it is now a watch item.
texts.text: The precast segment supplier reported a temporary reduction in weekly output caused by mould maintenance and labour shortage. This has not yet become a critical path failure, but it is now a watch item.

texts.text: Weekend and selected night concrete pours approved for critical viaduct fronts.

texts.text: Alternate span erection sequence developed to reduce weather exposure.

texts.text: Revised authority submission issued for telecom and water diversion crossing.

texts.text: Interface war room started with civil, systems, and employer representatives.
